_meta.title: Report/status component family (ui-table + ui-stat + ui-badge) — design INTAKE (scope ruling + first records; no build)
_meta.author: planner (design seat)
_meta.grounding: The verified forcing evidence: a2ui-catalog.spec.md §5.2 guidance routes 'exact values scanned row-by-row' to a List-composed table (an idiom papering over a missing type); the metric tile is the corpus's most-taught composition (patterns.ts:183-187, catalog-coverage.ts:319-323 + stats-grid-dashboard hand-compose caption+h3-in-card) with no component; status vocabulary (tag/badge/chip) sits in geometry.md's compact-realm roster, never ported. Laws: ADR-0107 (the intake shape + Display-class siblings) · ADR-0102 (three-lane chooser) · ADR-0087 (whole-fleet catalog gate, rows same-wave) · ADR-0097 + Amendment (feed partition is TOTAL) · ADR-0057 (non-color signifier; its own forward trigger names 'any badge/alert/toast/tag family') · references/geometry.md + ADR-0041 (compact realm) vs ADR-0038 (control lookup) · ui-text as-stamp doctrine (ADR-0078 cl.4).
_meta.note: Both planes derived independently: OUTSIDE-IN structure from the intake record family (scope ruling -> contract directions -> ecosystem dispositions -> records -> gates/handback — the ADR-0107 intake shape); INSIDE-OUT actions from the dispatch's per-type scope questions (types+fences, data shapes, rendering, a11y, geometry) + the standing doctrine set (ADR-0102 routing, SPEC-N2 same-wave gate, ADR-0097 total partition, handoff contract).

nodes.label: v1 scope ruling (three types in + a per-type fence, reasons from mechanics)
nodes.accept: the PRD scope section names ui-table/ui-stat/ui-badge as v1-IN AND the ruled-out set (sorting/selection/pagination/virtualization/resizing/cell-renderers · interactive chips/dismissal · anchored count-dots) each with a reason; harness_checks.py prd P3 passes as part of exit 0

nodes.label: contract directions (the what-direction ADR-0111 records; SPEC/LLD own mechanisms at build)
data contracts (prop shapes a model can emit as JSON, {path}-bindable, shallow-validation posture)
nodes.accept: ADR-0111 Decision states each type's prop names + shapes + one verbatim JSON example an agent could emit, citing the BarChart array-prop + shallow-item-schema precedent (catalog spec §5.2 BarChart row)

nodes.label: rendering directions (native-<table> stamp doctrine; real-DOM-text stat tile; compact badge token with component-drawn intent glyph)
nodes.accept: ADR-0111 Decision names the render mechanism per type; the table clause cites the ui-text as-stamp precedent (ADR-0078 cl.4) and the overflow-in-own-container law (ADR-0102 Lane A); no ARIA-grid-on-divs alternative survives outside Alternatives

nodes.label: a11y directions (what each type announces; the ADR-0057 posture on every intent/direction surface)
nodes.accept: ADR-0111 Decision states per type: table = native table semantics (th scope/caption) with no synthetic ARIA; stat delta direction = glyph+sign+announced text, never color alone; badge intent = co-carried non-color glyph per ADR-0057's checkable contract

nodes.label: geometry placements under the five-class taxonomy (+ the geometry.md badge dual-listing reconcile)
nodes.accept: ADR-0111 Decision places table/stat in Display and badge in the compact realm (ADR-0041 widget ramp + compact pad), names the --ui-{table,stat,badge}-* token families, and books the geometry.md dual-listing repair as a Repairs item

nodes.label: catalog + Gen-UI teaching disposition (SPEC-N2/ADR-0087 same-wave rows; the §5.2 four-way guidance RE-BASE; exemplar)
nodes.accept: ADR-0111 Decision states the same-wave catalog-row-or-allowlist path (end-of-wave residue: none), the §5.2 guidance re-base (Stat replaces 'metric tile', Table replaces 'List table' in the four-way rule), and names the exemplar upgrade that retires the hand-composed tile

nodes.label: feed-policy dispositions (ADR-0097 TOTAL partition — every new type owes IN or OUT + reason at build)
nodes.accept: ADR-0111 records a recommended disposition per type (Badge IN · Stat OUT · Table OUT) each with a reason grounded in the ask-vs-content split, states the bookkeeping is mechanically owed when rows land (the Amendment-2 lesson — never 'no edit owed'), and flags the Stat lean as a Kim fork

nodes.label: PRD sibling-vs-extension ruling + the compact PRD (.claude/docs/prd/report-family.prd.md)
nodes.accept: the PRD file exists under .claude/docs/prd/ with stable PRD-G IDs, metric+baseline+target+timeframe per goal, both-sided scope, priority tiers, and the sibling-vs-extension ruling in its header; harness_checks.py prd <path> exits 0

nodes.label: scoping ADR-0111 at the RESERVED number (proposed; Kim forks with firm recommendations)
nodes.accept: 0111-report-family-v1-scope.md exists under .claude/docs/adr/ with Status: proposed (never self-flipped — the hook enforces), non-empty Repairs, all four required sections + Acceptance, and >=2 named Kim forks each carrying a firm recommendation; adr_check.py <path> exits 0

nodes.label: index row (adr/README.md — appended carefully; concurrent siblings own 0112/0113-0114)
nodes.accept: adr/README.md index table carries a 0111 row (title + status + repairs) appended after a fresh re-read of the file; adr_check C6 (indexed) passes; no sibling row is disturbed

nodes.label: mechanical harness gates run to green
nodes.accept: coverage_check.py --strict exits 0 on this manifest AND harness_checks.py prd exits 0 AND adr_check.py exits 0 — all actually run this session with output read (never eyeballed)

nodes.label: design-status handback (handoff-compose contract)
nodes.accept: the final handback carries the eight handoff fields in order, Status on line 1, gate verdicts by command, and names doc-reviewer as the ratifying critic

actions.label: decide which report/status types earn v1 and fence the rest per type, argued from mechanics not fashion
decide each type's model-emittable data shape (table columns+record-rows vs array-of-arrays weighed; stat label/value/delta/caption; badge label+intent enum)
decide the rendering mechanism per type under fleet law (real native <table> stamp vs ARIA-on-divs; real-DOM-text tile; component-drawn intent glyph)
decide what each type announces to assistive tech + apply the non-color-signifier law to delta direction and badge intent
place the three types in the geometry taxonomy and reconcile geometry.md's badge dual listing
disposition the catalog rows + the guidance re-base + the exemplar under the whole-fleet gate
disposition the ADR-0097 ask-feed partition per type with recommendations and the build-owed bookkeeping named
rule PRD-extension-vs-sibling and author the compact PRD with gated goals
record the scope decision as proposed ADR-0111 naming the genuine Kim forks
index the new record so every citation resolves, without disturbing concurrent siblings' rows
run the mechanical doc gates and fix to green
compose the handback naming the reviewer seat

edges.why: the PRD's scope section pins the v1-in/out ruling — it cannot be authored before the ruling exists
ADR-0111's Decision clause 1 records the type-set + per-type fences
the ADR records the data-contract directions; deciding them is prerequisite content
the ADR records the rendering directions (the stamp call is load-bearing); deciding them is prerequisite content
the ADR records the a11y/ADR-0057 directions; deciding them is prerequisite content
the ADR records the geometry placements + the dual-listing repair; deciding them is prerequisite content
the catalog/guidance/exemplar disposition is ADR Decision content
the feed-partition dispositions (and the Stat fork) are ADR content
the ADR's Repairs field pins the PRD and its clauses cite PRD-G IDs — the PRD must exist first
the index row cites the ADR's number + title
the PRD gate runs over the authored PRD
the ADR gate runs over the authored ADR
adr_check C6 asserts the README index row exists — the gate can only pass after indexing
the handback reports gate verdicts by command — the gates must have run first
